name: Copenhagen (Denmark)

colors.symbolism: the heraldic field of the city's arms
the heraldic gules of the city's arms
the gold of the city's coat of arms

description: A white field with the coat of arms of Copenhagen at the centre: three white towers above blue waves between two red stars and a moon-faced figure.
history: The Copenhagen flag, displaying the city's medieval coat of arms (first attested in the seal of 1296), is in current use though never formally enacted by statute.

features.role: Coat of arms of Copenhagen: three towers above sea waves, between two stars and a man in the moon
features.symbolism: the three towers represent the medieval defences of Koebenhavn (the harbour); the stars and crescent moon refer to the city's medieval seal of c. 1296
